Ntc And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltfs Request For Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar May-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jason Lundberg, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: April Noble
David Semel, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Phuong Pham

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified. Plaintiff April Noble, Mike Conway and Donald Jingles' Motion to compel further responses to plaintiffs' request for [sic] form interrogatories and request for production of documents granted in part, denied in part, as follows:
1. Motion to compel further responses to plaintiffs' form interrogatories, within 20 days of this order, defendant shall provide verifications to his responses to plaintiff's form interrogatories;
2. Motion to compel further responses to plaintiffs' request for production of documents:
a. Granted as to request for production nos. 5, 7, 9, 10, 11, 13, 14, 20, 22, 25, and 27;
b. Granted as to request for production nos.2, 3, 4, 6, and 8; provided that defendant shall respond and produce documents limited to the period of his ownership, and any documents transferred to him at the time of acquisition of the property;
c. Denied, without prejudice to propounding another request, as to request for production nos. 1, 16, 17, 18, 19, 21, and 24.
d. Defendant shall provide verified supplemental responses within 20 days of the date of this order.
Request for sanctions is denied. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PHUONG PHAM'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Demurrer overruled as to the second cause of action regarding plaintiffs Noble and Conway, but sustained with ten days leave to amend regarding plaintiff Jingles. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/ARN)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
